seoTitle: How to Give Permission in Google Docs (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Grant sharing permissions in Google Docs by setting collaborators as Viewer, Commenter, or Editor, and control link access with the Share button.
intro: Controlling who can see or edit your document is one of the most common tasks in Google Docs. This guide covers using the Share button to add specific collaborators, choosing the right access role for each person, and adjusting general link-sharing settings so you keep tight control over your file's privacy.

steps.heading: Open the Share button
steps.body: Click the blue Share button in the upper right-hand corner of your document to open the sharing panel.

steps.heading: Add specific people
steps.body: Use the Share with people and groups field to enter email addresses for the collaborators you want to add.

steps.heading: Set the right access role
steps.body: Choose between Viewer for read-only access, Commenter for feedback without edits, or Editor for full modification rights.

steps.heading: Adjust general access
steps.body: Toggle General access between Restricted and Anyone with the link, depending on how broadly you want the document shared.

steps.heading: Review advanced settings
steps.body: Open the gear icon to restrict editors from changing permissions further or to stop viewers from downloading and copying your text.

steps.heading: Copy the link and confirm access
steps.body: Copy the secure link and review your list of active users to confirm privacy settings are correct before sending the file out.

faq.question: What's the difference between Commenter and Editor access?
faq.answer: Commenters can leave feedback and suggestions without changing the actual text, while Editors have full ability to modify the document directly.

faq.question: Can I stop people from downloading my document?
faq.answer: Yes, the advanced settings under the gear icon let you disable downloading, printing, and copying for viewers and commenters.

faq.question: How do I remove someone's access later?
faq.answer: Reopen the Share panel, find the person's name in the access list, and change their role to Remove access.

faq.question: Is Anyone with the link access safe to use?
faq.answer: It can be convenient for quick sharing, but it means anyone who obtains the link can access the document, so use it carefully for sensitive files.
